stem: A computer can present a Graphical User Interface (GUI) or a Command Line Interface (CLI).
model_answer: A GUI uses windows, icons, menus and a pointer (WIMP) so users interact mainly with the mouse. It is intuitive for beginners but uses more system resources. A CLI is text-only: users type commands into a terminal. It is faster for experienced users and uses very few resources, but has a steep learning curve and error messages are often terse. A typical school user prefers the GUI; a server administrator prefers the CLI for scripting and remote access.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award one accurate description of each interface type.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe one advantage of a GUI and one advantage of a CLI.

mark_points.text: GUI advantage: easy/intuitive to use without training (WIMP)
CLI advantage: faster for experienced users / scriptable / uses fewer resources
